title: Grimscar Expanded - Expansions Overview (Solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gAlQprevPh0

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns to Crowart's *Grimscar*, one of his favourite solo RPGs, to walk through the recent Kickstarter expansion wave. Physical editions of the new material aren't confirmed yet, but PDFs are live on itch.io and the zines are easy enough to print at home — Daniel has a separate video on that.

The headline addition is the new spiral-bound bestiary, presented as the writings of one Their Templeton. Stats sit alongside lore vignettes and — the surprise — small monster-hunting side quests issued by an NPC named Factory: slay the right beast, bring back a token, take the coin. Four further expansions (Deep Thicket, Black Lake, the Root Domain, the Burnt Glade) each extend the Dark Wield map with new biomes, NPCs, quests, and creatures. Dark Rites adds five city-bound quests inside Grimscar itself, and the Hidden Campaign offers swap-in hex entries and optional side-quest cards for players returning for a second or third pass.

Daniel's recommendation if you're forced to pick: the bestiary first. It adds the most to the game, and just reading it is a pleasure.

----

When a solo RPG's expansions split between new territories and a redesigned monster manual, which actually changes how you play more?
